title: Thoughts on 11/20/22 — Ruby Drama, Drinking, Casey Party
html:
TL;DR

  • Nov 2022 -style entry — written during the day around people, which Yahir flags as rare ("the intent of these entries is for no one to read them").
  • Names this term as a personal high-water-mark for alcohol consumption: not abusive but every weekend, with self-aware concern about "stooping down to levels that are embarrassing" and the cliche-creep of meaningful nights.
  • Documents the Ruby/Allie/Casey-party incident: Yahir had ghosted a girl named Ruby earlier in the term; ran into her at a party while flirting with her roommate Allie; while explaining the situation to friends David and Rowan, Ruby's coworker overheard the line "I'm talking to her to finesse with the hot roommates" and reported it to Ruby. Ruby ended up huddled crying with roommates; Yahir got blocked on Snapchat next day.
  • Self-assessment: "I am a dick for that... but also it in many ways is below my level of care." This is the dispassionate-with-flashes-of-conscience register that recurs in the corpus across romantic-fallout entries.
  • Closing turn — "please stay dialed, do not lose focus motherfucker, you got this" — the second person self talk that Yahir uses across the writing as feeling exit notes from 2018 onward.

Core content

Two threads run in parallel. The alcohol thread is observational and slightly nervous — "beer is an awesome way of bringing people together but it can also tear people apart" — written like Yahir is logging a behavior pattern he wants to keep an eye on rather than declaring intent to change. The Ruby thread is the actual narrative: ghost → spontaneous reconnect → flirt with the roommate → caught talking shit → social fallout.

The most load-bearing line for the wider corpus is the self-assessment register: "I am a dick for that... but also it in many ways is below my level of care." This is the early form of the disconnect-from-outcome posture (disconnected from outcome) Yahir later applies to business — but applied here to social/romantic blowback in a way that reads as both honest and slightly callous.

The friends present — David, Rowan, Henry — are inner-circle observers; this is also the first surfacing of "Henry" and "Rowan" in the friend-group log. The Casey in "Casey party" is a host name, not Chan Taylor adjacent.

Stub notes

  • ruby — queued to potential entities (single-doc romantic-fallout, no surname)
  • allie — queued to potential entities (roommate, single-mention)
  • rowan — queued to potential entities (friend, single-mention)
  • henry — queued to potential entities (friend, single-mention)

Connections

  • osu / corvallis or — location
  • david — existing entity in friend group
  • — form/genre
  • founder loneliness 2022 2026 — second-person self-talk pattern
  • recurring theme you havent named — writing-as-feeling-exit thread

related.label: There's a recurring theme in your writing you haven't named yet — writing-as-feeling-exit
